name: Chocolate Chestnut Bourbon Torte
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp Chilled butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the cake: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour two nine-inch cake pans. Beat all egg yolks well. Mix in 1 1/2 cups sugar, 4 tbsp bourbon, and 2 cups chestnut puree. Stir in ground pecans. Whisk egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff. Gently fold the white mixture into the yolk mixture. Divide batter evenly between cake pans and bake for 25 minutes. Let cool on a rack, then remove from pans.
2. Make the filling: Beat remaining 2 yolks with 1/3 cup sugar. Add instant coffee and 1 tablespoon hot water. Incorporate chilled butter until smooth. Add 2 tsp bourbon and remaining chestnut puree. Fold in chopped bitter chocolate. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
3. Melt bittersweet chocolate with heavy cream over low heat. Stir in 4 tbsp bourbon, transfer to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate.
4. Assemble the cake: Spread chilled chestnut filling on one layer of cake. Top with second layer. Cover torte with chocolate icing and sprinkle with pecan pieces. Chill before serving. Meanwhile, prepare bourbon chantilly for table service by whipping cream to soft peaks, gradually adding confectioners' sugar, then folding in 4 tbsp bourbon.
